item.title: AI Developer Radar｜Daily｜2026-06-11 - Today's Summary
item.description: The dominant pattern today is a sharp shift toward structured, production-grade agent skill packs and MCP-enabled tooling. addyosi/agent-skills exploded with 2,906 new stars in 24 hours (7.8x acceleration), signaling strong developer demand for pre-built, senior-level engineering workflows that can be injected into coding agents. Simultaneously, thedaviddias/Front-End-Checklist saw a sudden breakout (3.5x acceleration) after exposing its 385 rules via MCP, turning a static checklist into an agent-consumable resource. This convergence suggests the market is moving beyond raw agent frameworks toward curated, domain-specific skill libraries that agents can invoke. The Hacker News top post on Claude Fable 5 (2,562 votes) reinforces the theme of agent capability expansion, while a German ruling on Google's AI Overview liability adds a regulatory undercurrent. If this pattern holds, expect more static knowledge bases and checklists to gain attention by adding MCP interfaces. Top projects: thedaviddias/Front-End-Checklist, ChatGPTNextWeb/NextChat, NirDiamant/RAG_Techniques, stas00/ml-engineering, addyosmani/agent-skills, esengine/DeepSeek-Reasonix, x1xhlol/system-prompts-and-models-of-ai-tools, nexu-io/open-design, rohitg00/ai-engineering-from-scratch, tirth8205/code-review-graph, santifer/career-ops, HKUDS/nanobot Note: GitHub API 只提供当前 stars，总量变化来自本项目保存的历史 snapshot。 Note: daily delta 使用约 24h 前 snapshot，weekly delta 使用约 7 天前 snapshot。 Note: Accelerating 只展示至少 3 天历史基线且今日 delta 超过前 3 日均值 2 倍的项目。 Note: Potential Score 为规则评分，用于排序，不代表项目质量结论。

item.title: [sudden_breakout] thedaviddias/Front-End-Checklist (+20 stars/24h)
item.description: Category: AI Agent Framework Description: A comprehensive front-end quality checklist with 385 rules, now exposed via MCP for AI agents. Why it matters: With 72,871 stars and a sudden breakout acceleration of 2.3 (high), the project is experiencing a sharp uptick in attention. The 24h star delta of 18 and 7d delta of 82 indicate sustained interest, while the acceleration suggests a recent catalyst. The MCP server integration makes it immediately useful for AI agents, aligning with the current surge in agentic workflows. Developer insight: This signals a shift toward structured, machine-readable best practices that AI agents can consume directly. Developers should expect more projects to expose rule sets via MCP or similar protocols, enabling automated, context-aware code review. Risk: Low

item.title: [sudden_breakout] ChatGPTNextWeb/NextChat (+26 stars/24h)
item.description: Category: Local LLM / Inference Description: NextChat is a lightweight, cross-platform AI chat client that supports multiple LLMs (Claude, GPT-4, Gemini, DeepSeek) with one-click deployment and a privacy-first local-storage design. Why it matters: With 88,131 stars and a 24h star delta of 22, the project shows sustained high interest. The acceleration ratio of 2.9 (high) indicates a sudden breakout in attention, likely driven by recent feature additions like MCP support, real-time chat, and the iOS app release. The 7-day average star delta of 7.8 confirms consistent growth, but the acceleration signals a recent spike. Developer insight: The rapid adoption of NextChat signals a shift toward unified, self-hosted AI interfaces that reduce dependency on single providers. The MCP support hints at a future where AI tools interoperate via standardized protocols, opening opportunities for plugin ecosystems. Risk: Low

item.title: [sudden_breakout] NirDiamant/RAG_Techniques (+47 stars/24h)
item.description: Category: RAG / Knowledge Base Description: A comprehensive, community-driven collection of 42+ runnable Jupyter notebooks covering RAG techniques from basic to cutting-edge, with a companion book and active community. Why it matters: With 27,778 stars, a 24h delta of +32, and a 7d delta of +129, the repo shows strong sustained interest. The acceleration ratio of 2.1 indicates a sudden breakout, likely driven by the recent addition of 42 notebooks and active community engagement (Discord, Reddit, newsletter). Developer insight: The RAG landscape is maturing rapidly; having a curated, up-to-date reference like this is becoming essential for staying competitive. The community aspect (PRs welcome, Discord) signals a shift toward collaborative learning. Risk: Low

item.title: [sudden_breakout] stas00/ml-engineering (+7 stars/24h)
item.description: Category: RAG / Knowledge Base Description: A comprehensive open book of methodologies, tools, and commands for training and fine-tuning large language and multi-modal models. Why it matters: With 18,015 stars and a sudden acceleration ratio of 2.9 (high), the repo is experiencing a breakout. The 24-hour star delta of 20 and 7-day average of 6 indicate a sharp increase in attention, likely driven by the growing need for hands-on ML engineering guidance as LLM training becomes more accessible. Developer insight: Signals a shift toward open, community-driven knowledge sharing in ML engineering, reducing the barrier for smaller teams to adopt large-scale training practices. Risk: Low

item.title: [sudden_breakout] addyosmani/agent-skills (+2906 stars/24h)
item.description: Category: AI Agent Framework Description: A production-grade pack of 24 structured skills and slash commands that encode senior engineering workflows for AI coding agents. Why it matters: The repo has exploded from 0 to 51,894 stars with a 24h delta of 2,051 and a 7d delta of 3,844. The acceleration ratio is 4 (high), indicating a sudden breakout. This is not gradual growth — it's a viral spike driven by the release of a comprehensive, well-documented skill pack for multiple agent platforms. Developer insight: The project signals a shift from ad-hoc agent prompting to structured, skill-based agent programming. It suggests that the future of AI-assisted development lies in reusable, verifiable skill packs rather than raw LLM prompts. Developers should consider adopting or contributing to such skill ecosystems. Risk: Low

item.title: [sustained_hot] esengine/DeepSeek-Reasonix (+637 stars/24h)
item.description: Category: Coding Agent / SWE Agent Description: A DeepSeek-native AI coding agent for the terminal, rewritten in Go for performance and prefix-cache stability. Why it matters: With 20,994 stars, a 24-hour delta of +637, and a 7-day average of +527 stars per day, this project is experiencing sustained high growth. The acceleration ratio of 1.3 indicates steady, organic interest rather than a sudden spike. It has been consistently trending for weeks, suggesting strong community adoption. Developer insight: This signals a shift toward specialized, model-native coding agents that prioritize cost efficiency and session persistence. The plugin architecture (MCP-compatible) also points to an ecosystem play where tools are composable. Risk: Low

item.title: [sudden_breakout] x1xhlol/system-prompts-and-models-of-ai-tools (+463 stars/24h)
item.description: Category: Coding Agent / SWE Agent Description: A massive, community-curated collection of system prompts, internal tools, and AI models from dozens of popular AI coding assistants and platforms. Why it matters: The repo has exploded to 139k stars with a 24h delta of 278 and a 7d delta of 549, showing sustained high velocity. The acceleration ratio of 6.1 indicates a sudden breakout, likely driven by viral sharing across developer communities and social media. The star history chart shows a hockey-stick growth pattern, making it one of the fastest-growing AI repos on GitHub. Developer insight: The massive interest signals a strong developer appetite for transparency and customization in AI tools. Expect more open-source efforts to replicate or improve upon proprietary system prompts. Risk: Low

item.title: [sustained_hot] nexu-io/open-design (+726 stars/24h)
item.description: Category: Coding Agent / SWE Agent Description: Local-first, open-source alternative to Claude Design that turns your coding agent into a design engine with 150+ design systems, 260+ plugins, and multi-format export. Why it matters: With 63,116 stars, a 24-hour delta of +726, and a 7-day average of +685 stars/day, this project is sustaining massive, consistent growth. The acceleration ratio of 1.0 indicates steady organic momentum rather than a sudden spike. The recent 0.9.0 release (with built-in Model Router) and the Open Design Fellow program signal active development and community building. Developer insight: This signals a shift from AI as a chat interface to AI as a design engine integrated into existing developer toolchains. The BYOK proxy and MCP-first design mean any agent can become a design tool, reducing the need for specialized design UIs. The risk for incumbents like Figma is real — if agents can generate production-ready artifacts from a DESIGN.md, the canvas becomes optional. Risk: Low

item.title: [sustained_hot] rohitg00/ai-engineering-from-scratch (+377 stars/24h)
item.description: Category: AI Agent Framework Description: A massive, free, open-source curriculum (503 lessons, 20 phases) that teaches AI engineering from math foundations to production-grade agents, shipping a reusable artifact per lesson. Why it matters: With 31,049 stars, a 7-day delta of 3,156 (avg 1,931/day), and a 24h delta of 294, this repo is experiencing sustained explosive growth. The acceleration of 0.4 indicates high but stable velocity, not a sudden spike. The project has 150,639 readers and 241,669 page views in the last 30 days, signaling massive community adoption. It's trending because it fills a clear gap: practical, deep AI education that produces real artifacts. Developer insight: The market is shifting from API-wrapping to deep understanding. Developers who can build from scratch and ship production artifacts will have a significant advantage. This repo signals that the community values rigorous, hands-on learning over quick demos. Risk: Low

item.title: [sustained_hot] tirth8205/code-review-graph (+67 stars/24h)
item.description: Category: MCP / Tool Calling Description: Local-first code intelligence graph that slashes AI token usage by ~82x median during code reviews via MCP and CLI. Why it matters: With 18,337 stars, a 24h delta of +46, and a 7-day average of +76.2 stars/day, this project shows sustained high-velocity growth. The acceleration of 0.6 (moderate) indicates steady organic interest rather than a viral spike, making it a reliable tool for the AI coding ecosystem. Developer insight: This signals a shift toward local-first, context-aware AI tooling that minimizes API costs and latency. Developers building AI coding agents should consider integrating similar graph-based context pruning to stay competitive. Risk: Low

item.title: [sustained_hot] santifer/career-ops (+859 stars/24h)
item.description: Category: AI Workflow Automation Description: AI-powered job search system built on Claude Code. 14 skill modes, Go dashboard, PDF generation, batch processing. Why it matters: AI Workflow Automation 方向保持持续热门，24h stars +859，总 stars 52,569，适合看近期 release、commit 或社区讨论是否解释了增长。 Developer insight: 可以用一个最小样例验证 API、文档和集成成本，再决定是否纳入长期技术观察。 Risk: Low

item.title: [sustained_hot] HKUDS/nanobot (+67 stars/24h)
item.description: Category: AI Agent Framework Description: Lightweight, open-source AI agent for your tools, chats, and workflows. Why it matters: AI Agent Framework 方向保持持续热门，24h stars +67，总 stars 44,026，适合看近期 release、commit 或社区讨论是否解释了增长。 Developer insight: 可以用一个最小样例验证 API、文档和集成成本，再决定是否纳入长期技术观察。 Risk: Low
